_comment: Battle card for selling against Salesforce -- generated from evidence pools + reasoning synthesis
executive_summary: Salesforce CRM is experiencing accelerating churn pressure driven primarily by aggressive renewal pricing (15-25% increases) and admin-dependency creating single-point-of-failure risk. Decision-makers at mid-market companies (200-1000 employees) are most actively evaluating alternatives, with HubSpot capturing the largest displacement flow (67 confirmed switches in 90 days). The pricing wedge is strongest -- 42% of reviews mention cost complaints, and companies report $100K-$160K annual savings post-switch.
discovery_questions: When is your next Salesforce renewal, and have you seen the proposed pricing yet?
How many people on your team can actually administer Salesforce without outside help?
What percentage of the Salesforce features you're paying for does your team actually use?
If your Salesforce admin left tomorrow, how long would it take to replace them?
landmine_questions: Have you calculated the total cost of Salesforce ownership including admin salaries and consultant fees?
How much time do your reps spend on data entry versus actual selling each week?
When was the last time Salesforce shipped a feature that meaningfully changed how your team works?

objection_handlers.objection: We've invested too much in Salesforce integrations to switch
objection_handlers.acknowledge: That's a real concern -- integration re-platforming is the #1 hesitation we hear.
objection_handlers.pivot: But consider this: companies who switched report the integration work took 4-6 weeks on average, while they were paying $180K+ per year to maintain a system their team fights against daily.
objection_handlers.proof_point: Velocity Growth Partners had 12 Salesforce integrations and completed their HubSpot migration in 5 weeks, saving $160K annually.

objection_handlers.acknowledge: It's definitely the most recognized name in CRM.
objection_handlers.pivot: But 'industry standard' doesn't mean 'best fit.' 89 Salesforce customers in our dataset flagged active churn intent this quarter alone. The mid-market is moving.
objection_handlers.proof_point: HubSpot has captured 67 confirmed Salesforce switches in the last 90 days, primarily from companies with 200-1000 employees.

talk_track.opening: I've been analyzing CRM switching patterns across 847 Salesforce reviews from G2, Capterra, and TrustRadius. There's a clear trend: mid-market companies are hitting a pricing wall at renewal and finding that simpler alternatives deliver 80% of the value at 40% of the cost.
talk_track.mid_call_pivot: The interesting thing isn't that people are leaving -- it's why they stayed so long. The #1 anchor is integration switching costs. But when we look at actual migration timelines, they average 4-6 weeks. The perception of difficulty far exceeds the reality.
talk_track.closing: I can share the full intelligence brief on the Salesforce-to-HubSpot migration path -- it covers what 67 companies experienced, the common pitfalls, and the actual timeline. Would that be useful for your evaluation?

recommended_plays.key_message: Calculate your true Salesforce TCO including admin costs, consultant fees, and unused feature overhead
Your CRM shouldn't be a single-point-of-failure. What happens when your admin leaves?
recommended_plays.timing: Any time -- triggered by admin turnover signals

why_they_stay.summary: Integration switching costs and brand familiarity are the primary retention anchors. Teams that have heavily customized Salesforce face real re-platforming work.

strengths.evidence: Largest marketplace of third-party integrations in the CRM category
strengths.how_to_neutralize: Quantify the cost of maintaining those integrations versus the migration effort. Most companies use 5-10 integrations, not hundreds.

strengths.evidence: Enterprise procurement teams default to Salesforce as a 'safe' choice
strengths.how_to_neutralize: Show the churn data. 89 companies with active intent to leave this quarter alone. The 'safe' choice is getting expensive.
